content.en:
Write a friendly, professional parent newsletter for [month/period] for a [grade level] class at [school name or type].

Include these sections:
1. Welcome / opening message (2â€“3 sentences, warm tone)
2. What we've been learning (brief summary of 2â€“3 topics covered)
3. Upcoming events and dates (at least 3 items in a list)
4. How parents can help at home (2â€“3 practical, specific tips connected to current learning)
5. A spotlight moment or class achievement (one genuine positive highlight)
6. Contact information reminder

Tone: warm, inclusive, jargon-free. Length: one A4 page. Add [PHOTO SUGGESTION] where a class photo would fit.

content.en:
Prepare a structured set of talking points for a 15-minute parentâ€“teacher conference about a student named [student name / 'a student'] in [grade level] who [brief profile, e.g. 'is academically capable but struggles with focus and completing assignments on time'].

Organize into four parts:
1. Opening (30 sec) â€” friendly greeting, purpose of the meeting
2. Strengths (3â€“4 min) â€” 3 specific, evidence-based positive observations
3. Areas to develop (5â€“6 min) â€” 2â€“3 specific concerns, each paired with a concrete support strategy and a question to invite the parent's perspective
4. Action plan & next steps (3â€“4 min) â€” agreed goals, how school and home will collaborate, follow-up date

Include sample phrases for sensitive or difficult moments.

OrganizeazÄƒ Ã®n patru pÄƒrÈ›i:
1. Deschidere (30 sec) â€” salut prietenos, scopul Ã®ntÃ¢lnirii
2. Puncte forte (3â€“4 min) â€” 3 observaÈ›ii pozitive specifice, bazate pe dovezi
3. Zone de dezvoltat (5â€“6 min) â€” 2â€“3 preocupÄƒri specifice, fiecare cu o strategie de sprijin concretÄƒ È™i o Ã®ntrebare pentru a invita perspectiva pÄƒrintelui
4. Plan de acÈ›iune È™i paÈ™i urmÄƒtori (3â€“4 min) â€” obiective agreate, cum vor colabora È™coala È™i familia, data de follow-up

Include formulÄƒri exemplu pentru momentele sensibile sau dificile.

content.en:
Draft a professional, empathetic email to the parent of a [grade level] student regarding [situation, e.g. 'a noticeable improvement in participation this month' OR 'a concerning drop in grades and increased absences'].

The email should:
- Have a clear, non-alarming subject line
- Open with a brief, genuine positive observation about the student
- State the main point clearly and specifically (with one concrete example or data point)
- Propose a clear next step or call to action (e.g., a call, a meeting, trying a strategy at home)
- Close with an open, collaborative tone

Length: 150â€“200 words. Tone: professional but warm and solution-focused.
content.ro:
RedacteazÄƒ un e-mail profesional È™i empatic pentru pÄƒrintele unui elev din clasa [nivel] privind [situaÈ›ia, ex. 'o Ã®mbunÄƒtÄƒÈ›ire vizibilÄƒ a participÄƒrii Ã®n aceastÄƒ lunÄƒ' SAU 'o scÄƒdere Ã®ngrijorÄƒtoare a notelor È™i absenÈ›e frecvente'].

- AibÄƒ o linie de subiect clarÄƒ, care sÄƒ nu alarmeze
- ÃŽnceapÄƒ cu o observaÈ›ie pozitivÄƒ scurtÄƒ È™i autenticÄƒ despre elev
- Precizeze punctul principal clar È™i specific (cu un exemplu concret sau date)
- PropunÄƒ un pas urmÄƒtor clar sau un apel la acÈ›iune (ex. un apel telefonic, o Ã®ntÃ¢lnire, o strategie de aplicat acasÄƒ)
- Se Ã®ncheie cu un ton deschis È™i colaborativ

Lungime: 150â€“200 de cuvinte. Ton: profesional, dar cald È™i orientat spre soluÈ›ii.
